films.title: Frankenstein
films.description: So fun to watch these old horror films. Apparently back then they were scary as fuck! I love how the director is clearly gay because he made Frankenstein hot and smoulder into the camera a lot. The monster is great.
films.spoiler: I feel like the story is pretty different to what I knew. Note to self, read the original book.

films.title: The Boys
films.description: Great show, great take on a ridiculous concept: superheroes. It correctly identifies capitalism as the problem. So many horrible moments and this main character Homelander makes me want to throw up. Having finally reached the end of season 5 I do have to say that I'm glad it's over. It's so on the nose and the dialogue often pretty shit.

films.title: The Invisible Man
films.description: An old film about a man who turns himself invisible. Super entertaining. Lots to enjoy here. I'm loving classic films like this.
films.spoiler: I'm obsessed with how much of a diva this invisible man is!! And of course he goes around murdering people because he's finally free to take off his clothes, let his dick dangle and just be his gay self out in the open. It's like a gay fantasy. I explained to the group I was with that this is a gay film and when we looked up the director afterwards, yep he's gay. I didn't expect it but this is a camp classic.

films.title: Nightbreed
films.description: A very sexually charged and homoerotic horror action film where the money is invested more into the costumes and sets than the writing. Really funny.
films.spoiler: When she says 'I'll always stay with you!' then immediately screams and runs when she sees him. Hilarious.

films.title: Brazil
films.description: A dystopian 1984-type film which had some great characters and funny moments, but I honestly got a bit bored and started playing pokemon leaf green during the last hour or so.

films.title: What Ever Happened To Baby Jane?
films.description: A camp black and white film about a sour old lady who terrorises everyone around her. It's very entertaining and honestly quite shocking.

films.title: Pink Narcissus
films.description: A vibes-heavy 70's film about homoerotic imagery and sex fantasies without any dialogue. It's got great colours but I was really tired in the cinema and just wanted to go home.
films.spoiler: The scene with the dancer dancing with a fully erect cock was great. They really let that go on for a long time.

films.title: Scherbenland
films.description: An interesting glimpse of punk Berlin! It focused on a 70s punk band (great), a female singer-songwriter (great) and a group of three local lads who are doing Deutschrap (not great). The lads were cute because they are best mates, but they can't answer interview questions for shit and aren't exactly carrying the punk politics torch.

films.title: The Drama
films.description: An absolutely excruciating film in terms of second-hand embarrassment. All these rich Americans are deplorable.
films.spoiler: The twist is good. Loved all the references to 'shooting' really nailing how fucking awkward it all is. I think in his position I would call it off because Misha's right, it's psychopath behaviour. Insane position to be in though. The 'friend', Rachel, is the worst friend ever. And why does everyone immediately resort to alcohol whenever anything bad happens?

films.title: Only Good Things
films.description: Watched this at mongay. It has a vibes heavy first half that I really enjoyed, but I wasn't really on board with the tone shift in the second half.

films.title: Sirat
films.description: I will never forget this film. Watching it feels like a nightmare.
films.spoiler: I was sobbing in the cinema. It wasn't just me, people were crying all around me because it's just so fucking horrifying. The sheer shock and escalation is just astonishing. In the final moments I had my eyes closed and my fingers in my ears because I couldn't deal with the explosions. I sat in the seat long after the film ended and it took me a very long time to finally get on my bike to go home because I just felt so hollowed out and devastated. I will never forget the scene with the handbrake. As time has gone on, I've realised that there's more to this film that just the horrible things that happen. There's a lot of symbolism and meaning behind the horror.

films.title: A World Not Ours
films.description: A film about Ein el-Helweh, a refugee camp in Lebanon filled with Palestinians. Fascinating. For me it was a reminder how fucked it is that refugees can't work. In this camp they are trapped there and they don't do any proper work because they don't have the papers. All over the world it's the same story, asylum seekers twiddle their thumbs in places like the UK when they could be working, and they want to work. It's fucked.

films.title: Mar Mama
films.description: Upsetting short film about settler violence in the West Bank. Things like this are happening right now in Palestine. These horrible settlers are horrible and fuck the Israeli security in the region who support the settlers.

films.title: Vibrations from Gaza
films.description: Upsetting short film about deaf kids in Gaza pre-October 7. There's a school of deaf kids there and I was just asking myself, has it been bombed now?

films.title: The History of Sound
films.description: I was setting myself up to not enjoy this because *rolls eyes* the power of music. But actually I got totally swept away by it. Loved it. Beautiful.
films.spoiler: I already knew about the song 'Silver Dagger' from a Fleet Foxes cover that I adore. Then I heard the Tallest Man on Earth do a cover and say it's actually a traditional folk tune, popularised by Joan Baez. I was shook when they sang it at the start of the film, a song about saying no to men, better to die alone. And then when it came back at the very end of the film, I admit it I was sobbing uncontrollably in the cinema.

films.title: Pulp Fiction
films.description: A super masc, violent set of random stories with no real moral to the story. I watched it at home on my own for my education but didn't enjoy it.
films.spoiler: Honestly this film is so vacuous, it's about nothing. When I watch stuff like this I just think about the all the dudes who have seen this thinking that the characters are cool and want to be like them. I was reading about it on the Wikipedia page and apparently it's just a huge collection of references to other movies, which explains a lot.

films.title: The Mario Galaxy Movie
films.description: As a Nintendo fan I was contractually obliged to watch this, but it really is mainstream nonsense. It's a series of happenings and references with no real meaning. I did get every reference though, which was satisfying.
films.spoiler: As I sat in the huge, fancy Cinestar, looking at this huge, fancy film making all these references to all this Nintendo stuff that I loved, I asked myself, 'is this cool now?' This niche that I've enjoyed my whole life blown up on the big screen like this. Franz told me it was great because he felt like a child again and I see where he's coming from but I also feel like Nintendo has become big enough where all these nostalgia points are now marketable moments which can be converted into capital. The whole thing made me feel uneasy. Also, although they didn't use (much) licensed music, the generic-ass 'epic' orchestra renditions of mario music felt AI-generated. The whole film was kinda giving AI-generated.

films.title: Pillion
films.description: Wow, this is a steaming hot film about getting dommed by sexy biker man Alexander Skarsgård. It handles the topic excellently, is super funny, and I highly recommend it. Managed to get a free T-shirt from the Mongay preview!
films.spoiler: I loved the part where Colin throws a tantrum and you think he's gonna get fucking murdered by Ray, but instead Ray actually tries to do the 'day off' thing. You see his attempts to do something different, because he knows it's not good to just go full in on the sub-dom stuff when you actually end up with someone for a long time, when you fall in love with them. He makes an attempt, and of course he panics (that moment was hard to watch), and you get the feeling that Ray is going to need like 3 more failed relationships before he gets it. For Colin it was also a learning experience, he goes into his next relationship with a non-negotiable day-off every week and that my friends is growth. Fucking love it. It's a film about different kinds of relationships and the lessons you can learn from them.

films.title: No Other Choice
films.description: Had a solo trip to the cinema (Delphi Lux) while I was already feeling sad and upset and this just made it worse. It's an excellent film but wow, the plot really is upsetting for so many reasons.
films.spoiler: The way he convinced himself that the only way to keep his way of living is by murdering three people with this little mantra was so scary. The film makes you think about... what if everyone did that. If we all convinced ourselves to make 'evil' decisions. He even gets served up snapshots of other people's lives, the other applicants have so many of the same problems as him, and he still goes through with it. Incredible. At the end we get a picture of his happy ending. So many people laid off anyway. It's like, it's what he wanted, great for him, but what kind of picture does it paint for humanity, yknow. I left the cinema despairing for humanity.

films.title: Marty Supreme
films.description: Enjoyed returning to Kino International with Marf to watch this, although it's so intense from start to finish. I would be lying if I said I wasn't entertained, but god the main character is so insufferable. People like that make my skin crawl.

films.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
films.description: Got super stoned and watched this at home and loved it. Best martial arts ever. Excellent female characters.

films.title: Iván & Hadoum
films.description: I won tickets to see the Teddy Award winner at Mongay! Kitty and I went and it was lovely. A romantic story about a trans man in Andalucia. So great to see what it's like being a trans man, something I know not very much about.

films.title: Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery
films.description: Watched with this with the family but I wasn't blown away by it. Devout Christians are just too weird for me, like the parts where Josh O'Connor is being a 'good priest'. It was an appropriate film to watch with the family on holiday.

films.title: Only Rebels Win
films.description: Saw this at the opening of the Berlinale, there was a red carpet and stars wearing designer outfits and everything. The film was great. A reminder that it's great to not be a normie. Really loved it.

films.title: Drunken Noodles
films.description: I was a rolling my eyes a little at the supernatural aspects of this film. But it featured a great romantic moment between this guy and a man in his 70s.

films.title: It Was Just an Accident
films.description: Really great film with a horrible premise (it's about torture) but it's full of good people and a really thrilling story.
films.spoiler: I keep coming back to that final scene with the sound of the leg. At first I was like, oh no they should have killed him, now he's gonna get shot or something, but I guess actually it must just be his imagination. If I were him I would be haunted by the knowledge that he's still out there and I would be hearing that sound constantly. It's like a special kind of trauma.

films.title: Heated Rivalry
films.description: This outrageous show about gay ice hockey players features so much hot gay sex between muscular men that it's practically porn. I totally understand why this show is so popular with women! What I loved about it was that there was no major trauma.

films.title: Flash Gordon
films.description: Terrible film but it was fun to laugh at it for an evening. There were some great costumes. America was America-ing hard on this one.

films.title: Carrie
films.description: The first half of this classic film was unbearable to watch, but from the iconic moment at the school prom onwards we were just screaming 'Yeeees fuck em up!! Burn it all down!'. If only this is what happened when people treated women so badly in real life.

films.title: The Great Escape
films.description: The family has this on the TV at Christmas quite a lot. It was alright. Got me thinking if the Germans have some kind of equivalent.

films.title: Aftersun
films.description: Story of a dad taking his 11 year old daughter on holiday, trying to keep it together. I felt a real connection to him because he's my age. It all felt very real.
films.spoiler: The part when he didn't sing karaoke with her was so horrible. He was so stubborn. He will regret that for the rest of his life, I am so sure of it. In that horrible part where it just cuts to him crying in the hotel with his back to the camera, I feel like that's what he's doing, he's just kicking himself.

films.title: Nickel Boys
films.description: Very upsetting film about a segregated youth detention centre where horrible shit went down. POV camera the whole time makes it all feel like it's happening to you.

films.title: The Secret Agent
films.description: Vibes-heavy Brazilian thriller featuring (truly) the best female characters and the worst male characters. I struggled to keep up with the plot and the events towards the end felt a bit random.

films.title: Bugonia
films.description: Oh my god I loved this film so much. It was wild and hugely entertaining. Conspiracy theorist meets powerful CEO and the dialog between them is just perfect. Best ending ever.
films.spoiler: The part when she gets out of the car and goes back to the office. I was like, oh hell yes. Hell yes. I love that she was just like yeah fuck this. This planet is lost, let's just kill the humans.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.description: Such a great film. Thriller that takes you places! So many amazing moments and great characters. I'm going to chuckling about Christmas Adventurers for a while now.

films.title: Frankenstein
films.description: They decided to make the monster hot and I don't understand why! I thought it was all pretty dumb. I guess it was entertaining.

films.title: Absolutely Fabulous
films.description: Love love love this show. Patsy is everything. It's so outrageous and camp and funny and I'm obsessed. Some stuff has aged very badly but it's worth it.

films.title: Pink Flamingos
films.description: Outrageous camp classic whose main goal is to be gross and horrible. It's hilarious and shocking and I have to say I preferred 'Female Trouble'. As time has passed I keep thinking about when she gives her political views. Iconic.
films.spoiler: Kill! Everyone! Now! Condone first degree murder! Advocate cannibalism! Eat SHIT!

films.title: Amrum
films.description: A gorgeous-looking film set during the German surrender in WW2 in a teeny-tiny island in north Germany. Fascinating glimpse into a time and place that I've never seen before. Simple/low-key plot but still engaging.

films.title: Painted Boats
films.description: Read about it in the Stoke Bruerne narrowboat museum when we had our boating holiday. Cool history lesson about life on canalboats before it became a tourism thing.

films.title: Moonstruck
films.description: We watched this because it has Cher in it but were horrified when the heart-throb man on the film turned out to be horrible Nicolas Cage. Cher is beautiful but the film aged badly.

films.title: Blue Boy
films.description: Short film featuring escorts listening to themselves tell stories. It was nice and thought-provoking.

films.title: Pornomelancolía
films.description: An upsetting film about making porn in Mexico. On paper things seem alright but you can tell he is deeply upset throughout most of the film. Largely real as well, according to the director FAQ afterwards, not just fiction.

films.title: Ciurè
films.description: ARGH this film really pissed me off. They went way too far with the straight guy. I disliked so many of his scenes, and how Ciurè just dealt with his bullshit at great cost for no reason. She was portrayed as a flawless human, but real people aren't like that. I liked that she looks like Amy Winehouse though.

films.title: Volver
films.description: Very tragic film. Classic Almodovar in the sense that it's about women looking beautiful and suffering. I love how competent and beautiful Penelope Cruz is despite her suffering.

films.title: Four Mothers
films.description: Delightful and hilarious film about a gay man looking after a bunch of old irish ladies. So fun and light-hearted. I enjoyed that queerness just simply isn't an issue in the film.

films.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
films.description: A lesbian longing film, featuring this actress just staring at the camera for most of it. I thought it was boring. Like surely in those times people didn't talk with extended silences like that.

films.title: All We Imagine As Light
films.description: Imagine you have an arranged marriage then your husband fucks off leaving you in Mumbai to live and work alone your whole life. This lady with bags under her eyes has accepted her fate and this film really hones in on that. A slow burner and a look into a different world.

films.title: If I Die, It'll Be Of Joy
films.description: Very cute film about an awkward topic: old age. It follows three fantastic old queer people and makes me both fearful and hopeful for old age.

films.title: Tchindas
films.description: Beautiful and positive film about the preparations for the carnival in Cape Verde. It follows a community of queer people not just living their best lives but being the centre of attention with no problems and no homophobia. Delightful.

films.title: I am a Flower
films.description: A cute and fun animated short about a flower man who is asking his mum if she's gonna be there for his transformation tomorrow. Heartbreaking story about parents who will just never understand. I cried at this one, and when the director gave a Q&A right after he cried too.

films.title: Speaking Flowers
films.description: Hilarious short film with lots of shots of plants and toy animals and occasionally this beautiful flower lady. I would like to purchase this film so I can consume it at a later date with friends while taking mushrooms.

films.title: Filament Fortune
films.description: Short film about three people living with HIV and how they deal with it. Some interesting thoughts, and it features this song by Jill Scott 'He loves me' which I really like.

films.title: The Flowers Stand Silently, Witnessing
films.description: Blown away by this old colour footage of white people picking flowers in Palestine in the early 20th century. The film-maker's rage and grief is palpable as they desperately look at the barely 2 minutes of footage containing Palestinian arabs. Heartbreaking and shocking and it really is just footage of flowers.

films.title: Baradiya
films.description: Film about a filipino trans woman accepting her role as shaman of her indigenous tribe. It features an amazing CGI sequence with very Avatar Last Airbender vibes.

films.title: Westler
films.description: Secretly filmed in the DDR all about a romance between these blue eyed very gay-looking boys. It was cool because of the historical aspect but also a huge snoozefest. The director was there at the cinema and wouldn't stop talking.

films.title: Oslo Stories: Sex
films.description: Third film in this trilogy of Oslo films and I'm so glad I got to see them all. This one has equally as many vibes as the other two, has a beautiful premise and is funnier than the others. Highly recommend.
films.spoiler: What is going on with that weird dance sequence at the end though!

films.title: Motel Destino
films.description: Spent the whole film ogling at the beautiful Brazilian man. Thrilling and quite silly, it features an actual queer mafia, and loads of strong coloured lighting. Very steamy film.

films.title: Mujeres al Borde de un Ataque de Nervios
films.description: A classic Almodóvar film. I loved it! These camp, hilarious Spanish films know exactly how to tickle me. The outfits in particular are great. Highlight was when she hijacked a motorbike and walked through the airport with her hair in a huge mess to kill her ex husband.

films.title: Oslo Stories: Dreams
films.description: Beautiful film about a girl who falls in love with a teacher, writes about it, and shares it with both her mother and grandmother, who have varying and changing perspectives on it. It's so raw and beautiful. And it links to Oslo Stores: Love as well, which I also loved.

films.title: Les Reines du Drame
films.description: Extremely camp lesbian dramady which is hugely entertaining and hilarious. For me it would have been a perfect score if it weren't for the songs, I don't think I will ever like musicals. But the film is still so good.

films.title: Dan Savage's Hump Porn Film Festival 2025 Part 1
films.description: A collection of porn films 5 minutes or less, for a second time! I can't deny that it's excellent. A couple of them truly horrified me. One of them super piqued my interest.

films.title: Sebastian
films.description: A mongay about a gay escort who writes himself into his own book with a pseudonym. He constantly looks like he's about to start crying throughout the film. I found it to be quite predictable and not all that impactful.

films.title: Possession
films.description: Amazing film, very intense, lots of very jarring cuts. Two very queer-coded side characters. Lots of quotable moments and an iconic scene in our very Platz der Luftbrücke!

films.title: Oslo Stories: Love
films.description: Beautiful soundtrack and stunning vistas of Oslo. Great vibe in this film. Very cosy and heartwarming. Some interesting ideas around relationships and the main female character is so beautiful. I really loved it.

films.title: Suspiria
films.description: Funny horror film with a witchy vibe. I really enjoyed it. One remarkable thing about it was the huge of these brightly coloured lights. Highly recommend.

films.title: Beyond the Valley of the Dolls
films.description: Wow I loved this film so much. It's a masterpiece. It's also a total clusterfuck of insane editing, quotable moments, wild group acting, very upsetting topics, comedy, queer nonsense, camp and a genuinely great all-female rock and roll band. Iconic film, can't wait to watch it again.
films.spoiler: I will say the imagery of the gun in the mouth was kinda upsetting

films.title: La Ley Del Deseo
films.description: Such a delightful Spanish film from the 80s full of gay sex, a young and hot Antonio Banderas, and the most amazing trans character ever. This film is so fun and so wild, with great music.

films.title: Adolescence
films.description: Very topical and very well done short drama about a young boy radicalised by the misogynist right. Made me very emotional. I'm grateful it became so popular so more people know about what's happening on our phones.
films.spoiler: The scene with the therapist when you see what's really going on in his head, horrible. I genuinely think this shit is real and happening in real time right now with all these kids who have phones. The currently generation of kids really are fucked. I don't think all the parents of the world are teaching them about echo chambers and misinformation online. The parents are getting radicalised just as well! Well, at least the show is successful and it started some important conversations in Britain.

films.title: Baby
films.description: A mongay film about sex workers in Sao Paolo which was kinda entertaining and had really beautiful 'chosen family' moments.

films.title: Flow
films.description: It's totally unrealistic for these animals to have this human-like reasoning and deduction!! But I forced myself not to think about that stuff because it's not actually about that. It's about the vibe. This film has no words in it and that is a vibe.

films.title: Dear Kelly
films.description: As a fan of Channel 5, a top class youtube channel, I decided to check out their feature film about this right-winger and how he came to be so lost. It's pretty good.

films.title: Nosferatu
films.description: Hilarious film, one of the OG horror films where a bunch of tropes come from. I love how Count Orlok travels across Europe in a coffin via raft just so he can suck a German lady's blood. Also this vampire stuff is so sexually charged it's insane.

films.title: Female Trouble
films.description: Absolutely insane film, there are no words to describe it. It goes way too far and is hugely inappropriate and very disgusting. I loved it and was hugely entertained for the entire thing.

films.title: Severance
films.description: I enjoyed this show but the story is so wild that you have to take a step back and just appreciate it for its vibes. The whole thing is so unrealistic! All the characters are bat-shit insane and the whole cult aspect makes me facepalm. Still I can't deny this show has great moments.

films.title: Lesbian Space Princess
films.description: Mongay always do a showing of the winner of the Teddy Award and this year it was this wacky animated film about lesbians in space. At parts it's hilarious but I did roll my eyes a few times.

films.title: Ato Noturno
films.description: Gripping, amazing film about public figures exploring their kink of sex in public. Cheesy film noir soundtrack makes it so funny and enjoyable to watch. I watched it at the Berlinale and I loved it.

films.title: Neosagradxs
films.description: My friends Clarer and Miiel made this film!! And I'm in it!! I have two lines speaking in spanish and they are terrible, I am a terrible actor. My apologies to the directors and the viewers.

films.title: B.O.Y.
films.description: I was so nervous during all of this film because it's so tense but the ending was so beautiful. A Danish queer coming of age film, not an easy watch.

films.title: The Substance
films.description: So good. Also the most horrible film I've ever watched. I'm not good with body horror so I'm not sure what I was doing watching this.

films.title: It Ends With Us
films.description: Pretty people gazing at each other for two hours, I didn't love it

films.title: Summer of 85
films.description: Standard gay film, was pissed off by one of them randomly acting like a dickhead at the end with no warning. Also the British girl is just ridiculous.

films.title: Coco
films.description: Rewatched this delightful film with Mexicans asking if it's all real and they were like yep. Actually a delightful film.

films.title: Slumdog Millionaire
films.description: Really enjoyed rewatching this classic. This film was the first proper look at India that we Brits got.

films.title: Pretty Woman
films.description: Rewatched this banger film. Great soundtrack and great vibes. I would also sell my body for Richard Gere.

films.title: Wallace and Gromit: Vengeance Most Fowl
films.description: Delightful kids film with lots of funny tropes and references. The narrowboat scene was great.

films.title: Le Comte de Montecristo
films.description: Surprisingly great! Some nonsense in there but is there anything really quite like revenge porn?

films.title: El Senador
films.description: Hilariously bad film set in Brazil, watched at Mongay. Acting so bad it's good. Great moment when the cinema audience gasped at the twist.

films.title: Paprika
films.description: Watched this anime film while taking mushrooms thinking it would be trippy and fun but it was just a series of dream sequences without much connecting story. Frustrating watch with a really unpleasant sexual assault scene.

films.title: The Blair Witch Project
films.description: Watched this while taking mushrooms thinking it would be scary but it was actually mainly suspenseful and fun. Too many Americans, I didn't really enjoy it.

films.title: Anora
films.description: Fascinating, thrilling film about a sex worker. I was on the edge of my seat the whole time, highly recommend.

films.title: Sunflower
films.description: At Mongay, I love sunflowers but this is just trope-y bullshit with cuts to a sunflower field. Very plain.

films.title: Mamantula
films.description: Horror film about a man-spider who eats people after sucking their dicks. Investigation by Spanish speaking lesbian detectives in Berlin. So good for so many reasons, loved it so much.

films.title: The Deep Queer Massacre
films.description: Hilarious horror film with a bunch of gays getting picked off one by one in the countryside by an evil trans lady. Loved it.

films.title: Des Garçons de Province
films.description: Pretty plain gay film at Mongay but I enjoyed the different vibe to your standard gay film.

films.title: The Boys
films.description: Great show, great take on a ridiculous concept: superheroes. It correctly identifies capitalism as the problem. So many horrible moments and this main character Homelander makes me want to throw up.

films.title: Smiley Face
films.description: Hilarious stoner comedy, a bit on the nose but great to watch someone just being so utterly distressed by events. Loved it.

films.title: Oldboy
films.description: Pretty wild film about being imprisoned for many years and finally getting out. Pretty grim, also pretty silly.

films.title: Dan Savage's Hump Porn Film Festival 2024
films.description: A collection of 25 porn films 5 minutes or less. Fantastic, changed my whole outlook on sex. Life changing.

films.title: Milk and Serial
films.description: Very unpleasant watch, about youtube pranksters, a topic I struggle with.

films.title: And Then We Danced
films.description: What’s it like to be gay as a Georgian traditional dancer? All the usual tropes.

films.title: Patagonia
films.description: Gay film in Italy pretty different to your average Mongay. Has a wild scene where he pisses on him.

films.title: Love Lies Bleeding
films.description: Watching this with a cinema full of lesbians was hilarious. It's brutal and hilarious.

films.title: Crossing
films.description: Amazing and eye opening story about a Georgian women meeting a community of trans women in Istanbul. Highly recommend.

films.title: Problemista
films.description: Silly film about a difficult person and how hard it is to emigrate to the US. Light hearted and easy to watch. A pleasure.

films.title: Lose Your Head
films.description: Set in Berlin! Wasn't too bad. About gays and murder.

films.title: The Wedding Banquet
films.description: Hilarious, wild film about a man in a gay relationship marrying a woman for a green card.

films.title: The Women
films.description: Old film about women fawning around, it was pretty entertaining.

films.title: Challengers
films.description: Very sexually charged film about tennis. I found it a bit cringe but it had its moments.

films.title: Like it is
films.description: Interesting older gay film with some brutal scenes. Pretty forgettable.

films.title: Le Paradis
films.description: Pretty boring gay film about a youth detention centre in France. One cool part where he freestyle rapped but even that felt out of place.

films.title: Liuben
films.description: A sad gay film about Roma people in Bulgaria. Eye opening.

films.title: Love is the Devil
films.description: Film about Francis Bacon, hated it and hate him.

films.title: A Strange Way of Life
films.description: Short film about gay cowboys. Pedro Pascal's in it and it's fucking great.

films.title: The Human Voice
films.description: Short film with Tilda Swinton being dramatic on the phone. It's so good.

films.title: Anatomy of a Fall
films.description: Thrilling, gripping film about a murder trial in France. Insane.

films.title: La Huella de unos Labios
films.description: Sexy Mexican film about an alternative covid pandemic reality. Interesting but also pretty random.

films.title: All Shall be Well
films.description: Stunning film about older lesbians in Hong Kong. I cried and cried. I saw this because it won the Teddy Award prize at the Berlinale but it never fucking got distributed so I can't even recommend it to friends.

films.title: Wikiriders
films.description: Arty film about looking for rich guys in Mexico. The film feels unfinished because they apparently met him but couldn't show it for some reason.

films.title: Miller's Girl
films.description: Bad film about an older guy and how he fancies a minor. So icky and horrible.

films.title: All of Us Strangers
films.description: Loved this film so much. A gay man's hot new relationship with Paul Mescal and his old relationship with his suspiciously young parents. Love love love.
films.spoiler: The reveal! The way that you spend the whole film trying to figure out why the parents are so young and what the hell is going on. And then when he starts doing it with the new bf as well. Wow. One of the best twists ever. I still think about this.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.description: What a snooze fest. This film is for people who think they're smart. They all speak too quickly and it's too long.

films.title: In Bed
films.description: Really good film set almost entirely in a bedroom during a dramatic time about a hot gay guy who spirals downwards.

films.title: Horseplay
films.description: Deeply harrowing film about lads on holiday. Explains the deep rooted homophobia in Argentina.

films.title: Wir Werden Unsterblich Sein
films.description: Hilariously bad film from Saarland. A gay thriller with terrible acting and editing.

films.title: Arrête Avec tes Mensonges
films.description: Boring film about an old french famous person who used to love a man.

films.title: Golden Delicious
films.description: Cheesy American gay film nonsense.

films.title: Where Butterflies Don't Fly
films.description: Gay schoolteacher and pupil trapped in a cave together. Really well handled and suspenseful, enjoyable film.

films.title: The Beast in the Jungle
films.description: Cool view into a Paris nightclub over the years but didn't take away much else from this film.

films.title: Burning Days
films.description: Small town Turkey with a pretty interesting plot. I enjoyed it. Gay themes.

films.title: Fallen Leaves
films.description: Beautiful film about poverty in Finland. Has a heavy 60s/70s vibe but is in the present day.

films.title: Rotting in the Sun
films.description: Film about gay expats in Mexico, definitely saw myself in it. It was hilarious and wild. Loses tension a bit in the second half.
films.spoiler: I watched this literally right after visiting Zipolite and CDMX myself and let me tell you I felt called out!!! They really nailed it. My guilt of being a rich white boy in Mexico transformed into a film.

films.title: Passages
films.description: Like it's good, but I didn't enjoy it. Something about films with unlikeable main characters.

films.title: Drifter
films.description: This is about a boring countryside gay man becoming a Berlin queer. I really saw myself in him.
films.spoiler: It's a film about going from masc-for-masc gay of the Kleinstadt to the queer gay of Berlin. The before him is the gay that doesn't have female friends and the after him is the gay that listens to trans people. Show this film to people who don't understand what the word 'queer' means.

films.title: Heartbeats
films.description: Film about a gay guy and a girl falling in love with the same hot guy. Some hilarious moments, highly enjoyable.

films.title: Concerned Citizen
films.description: A film about gay men gentrifying a poor neighbourhood in Israel which hit me hard because I feel strongly about this topic.

films.title: Firebird
films.description: What's it like to be gay in the Russian military, except they don't even speak Russian!

films.title: Asteroid City
films.description: Silly film leaning heavily into the vibes but was actually kinda boring to watch.

films.title: Mon Crime
films.description: Fantastic lesbian nonsense! Delicious french film about women being successful.

films.title: Bis ans Ende der Nacht
films.description: Cool film about a trans woman and an interesting plot. Also quite forgettable.

films.title: Große Freiheit
films.description: This film totally stayed with me, even though the Austrian characters were hard to understand without subtitles. At the end when he goes cruising is very hard hitting.

films.title: The Swimmer
films.description: What's it like being gay in an Israeli swimming team? Not that inspiring.

films.title: El Houb
films.description: Devastating esposé on homophobic Arabic culture in Morocco. A strong willed man is determined to make his family understand that gay is fine.

films.title: The Super Mario Movie
films.description: Loved it. As a Nintendo fan this was a must watch. I mean, it's a bad film, yes. Why the fuck did they have licensed music in it. But come on it's mario.

films.title: Roter Himmel
films.description: Mongay film where the main dude is so unlikeable and so rude to the other characters that it affects the enjoyment of the film.

films.title: The Whale
films.description: Devastating film. Ugly sobbing in the aeroplane. Watching him shovel in the food in such a self destructive way as a response to his self hatred is so painful.

films.title: The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent
films.description: So cringe but I have to admit that I laughed a lot. It's a film about Nick Cage.

films.title: Beasts Clawing at Straws
films.description: Korean thriller, it was good but I was sleepy on a plane watching it. Not very memorable.

films.title: Tár
films.description: Great music, compelling story. Weird and uncomfortable world of conducting orchestras. The film is way too long.

films.title: Orlando, My Political Biography
films.description: So boring, a theatre type film about Virginia Woolf and trans people.

films.title: Dipped in Black
films.description: Kinda boring depiction of transness in native American communities.

films.title: Past Lives
films.description: Great opening scene. Loved humanising the American ‘villain’ character. Memorable film.

films.title: Futur Drei
films.description: Delightful film about second generation Iranian kids in Germany. Eye opening.

films.title: The Blue Caftan
films.description: Lovely film set in Morocco about embroidery. Just lovely. Quite long.

films.title: The Menu
films.description: Didn't understand the point of this film. Why did she order a burger and why was it such a big deal.

films.title: The Banshees of Inisherin
films.description: Deeply upsetting film about depression in rural Ireland. Very beautiful.
films.spoiler: When the finger went off I lost it.

films.title: Lonesome
films.description: A bit over the top gay film set in Australia. This was my second ever Kino-cumshot.

films.title: In from the Side
films.description: Fun film about a gay rugby team, but which featured absolutely no homophobia. It gets a high score from me because the main dude is so hot.
